title: robots.txt returns 404

evidence.observed: GET /robots.txt returns HTTP 404; no robots.txt file is available for https://sarconx.com/.

expected: A valid robots.txt at /robots.txt should exist, allow crawling, and reference the XML sitemap.
recommendation: Create /robots.txt with 'User-agent: *\nAllow: /\nSitemap: https://sarconx.com/sitemap.xml'. If canonical domain is sarconx.it, host and reference it there.

verification.assertion: GET /robots.txt returns 200 with crawlable directives and sitemap reference

expected_impact.rationale: Missing robots.txt prevents directive control and sitemap discovery, reducing crawl efficiency.

title: Canonical href points to a different registered domain

evidence.observed: Page served from https://sarconx.com/ (definitive production domain) declares <link rel="canonical" href="https://sarconx.it/">. DNS resolution for sarconx.it fails (NXDOMAIN); the canonical domain does not exist.

expected: Canonical URL should reference the definitive production domain https://sarconx.com/, or the non-existent sarconx.it domain should be abandoned/redirected.
recommendation: Update canonical link, og:url, and all schema @id/url properties to https://sarconx.com/. Remove or redirect any references to sarconx.it.

verification.reproduce: curl -s https://sarconx.com/ | grep -E "rel="canonical"|property="og:url"" && host sarconx.it || echo "sarconx.it does not resolve"

expected_impact.rationale: Cross-domain canonical splits authority and may cause the staging URL to be dropped from the index; AI systems may cite an unreachable domain.

title: Internal path links point to non-existent pages

evidence.observed: Single-page site contains root-relative links to /portfolio, /agent-operativita, /automazione-ai, /privacy-policy, /cookie-policy, /termini-condizioni, /siti-web, /software-dedicati, /gestionali, /app-web-app, /agentizzazione that return 404.

expected: Every internal link should resolve to an existing resource with HTTP 200.
recommendation: Create the linked pages (especially legal pages) or convert links to in-page anchors. At minimum /privacy-policy, /cookie-policy and /termini-condizioni must exist for a local-business site.

verification.assertion: All root-relative internal links return HTTP 200
verification.reproduce: for path in /portfolio /privacy-policy /cookie-policy /termini-condizioni; do curl -o /dev/null -s -w "${path}: %{http_code}\n" https://sarconx.com$path; done

expected_impact.rationale: Broken links waste crawl budget, create soft-404 experiences, and harm trust for local-business YMYL signals.

title: Page returns HTTP 200 with no redirect chain

evidence.observed: GET / returns HTTP 200 OK with 0 redirects.

expected: Target URL should return 200 and not chain through redirects.
recommendation: Maintain current 200 response for the canonical URL once domain strategy is resolved.

expected_impact.rationale: A clean 200 response ensures crawlers can fetch and index the page without redirect latency.

title: Meta robots allows indexing and following

evidence.observed: <meta name="robots" content="index, follow, max-snippet:-1, max-image-preview:large, max-video-preview:-1"> is present.

expected: Page should be indexable and links should be followed unless intentionally blocked.
recommendation: Keep as-is; revisit only if staging origin should be noindexed.

verification.assertion: robots meta contains 'index, follow' and no 'noindex'

expected_impact.rationale: Correct robots directives permit indexing and link discovery.

title: Primary service cards and headings are injected by JavaScript

evidence.observed:
Services slider container
is empty in raw HTML. Six service cards, H3 headings, descriptions and internal links are built from a JS array and injected at runtime.

expected: Core content, headings, and internal links should be present in static HTML so crawlers and non-JS clients can access them.
recommendation: Server-side render the service cards inside #serviceTrack; use JS only to enhance slider behaviour.

verification.assertion: raw_html contains service card H3 elements inside #serviceTrack
verification.reproduce: grep -n 'serviceTrack' index.html && grep -n 'Automazione Flussi AI' index.html

expected_impact.rationale: Search engines and AI crawlers that do not execute JS may miss primary service descriptions, reducing relevance coverage.

title: Page is primarily static HTML with no CSR hydration markers

evidence.observed: No __NEXT_DATA__, data-reactroot, ng-version, data-vue, or svelte hydration markers found.

expected: Static HTML should contain main content without requiring client-side rendering frameworks.
recommendation: Maintain static architecture; only fix the JS-injected service section.

expected_impact.rationale: Static HTML is easier and faster for crawlers to process than fully CSR pages.

title: Title tag exceeds recommended length

evidence.observed: <title>SarconX — AI Automation Agency Pavia & Milano | Agenti AI per Aziende | +300% Efficienza</title> is 88 characters.

expected: Title should be concise, front-loaded with primary keyword, and ideally within 50–60 characters.
recommendation: Shorten to roughly 55 characters, e.g. 'SarconX | AI Automation Agency Pavia & Milano'. Move value propositions to meta description.

expected_impact.rationale: Over-long titles truncate in search results, reducing click-through rate and diluting keyword prominence.

title: Meta description exceeds recommended length

evidence.observed: Meta description is 223 characters.

expected: Meta description should be 150–160 characters to display fully on most devices.
recommendation: Rewrite to 150–160 characters while preserving primary keywords and CTA.

verification.reproduce: node -e "const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const m=h.match(/<meta name=\"description\" content=\"(.*?)\"/); console.log(m[1].length)"

expected_impact.rationale: Descriptions longer than ~160 characters are truncated in SERPs, weakening snippet message and likely lowering CTR.

title: Core head hygiene elements are present

evidence.observed: Charset UTF-8, viewport, language, robots, description, canonical, and inline SVG favicon are all present.

expected: Head should contain charset, viewport, language, title, description, canonical, and favicon.
recommendation: Maintain current setup; only fix canonical/domain mismatch and title/description lengths.

expected_impact.rationale: Complete head hygiene supports correct rendering, indexing, and snippet presentation.

title: Viewport meta tag is correctly configured

evidence.observed: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> is present.

expected: Viewport should enable responsive scaling on mobile devices.
recommendation: Keep as-is.

expected_impact.rationale: Correct viewport is a prerequisite for mobile usability.

title: Small 10 px font sizes are used in multiple places

evidence.observed: The utility class text-[10px] appears 34 times for labels, badges, sources, footer metadata, and stat captions.

expected: Base readable text should generally be at least 12–16 px to meet mobile usability guidelines.
recommendation: Increase small labels to at least 12 px (text-xs). Reserve 10 px only for non-essential decorative elements.

expected_impact.rationale: Very small text can trigger mobile-usability warnings and is harder to read for users with low vision.

title: Heading hierarchy is logical with a single H1

evidence.observed: One H1 followed by H2 sections, then H3 cards/steps, and H4 footer columns. No skipped levels in static HTML.

expected: Single descriptive H1 and sequential heading levels.
recommendation: Preserve hierarchy; ensure JS-injected service cards use H3 in rendered DOM.

expected_impact.rationale: Clear heading structure improves accessibility and helps search engines understand content sections.

title: Open Graph and Twitter Card tags are implemented

evidence.observed: og:type, og:url, og:title, og:description, og:image, og:locale, og:site_name, twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, and twitter:image are all present.

expected: Social card tags should be present for sharing previews.
recommendation: Keep tags; fix og:url domain mismatch and add image dimensions + twitter site handle.

expected_impact.rationale: Complete social tags enable rich previews when the URL is shared.

title: Open Graph URL points to a different domain than the served page

evidence.observed: og:url is set to https://sarconx.it/ while the page is served from the definitive domain https://sarconx.com/ and sarconx.it does not resolve.

expected: og:url should reference the definitive, shareable URL https://sarconx.com/.
recommendation: Update og:url to https://sarconx.com/ and verify og:image is hosted on sarconx.com.

expected_impact.rationale: A mismatched og:url can cause social platforms to attribute shares and engagement to the wrong domain.

title: Social image URL cannot be verified at this tier

evidence.observed: og:image and twitter:image reference https://sarconx.it/og-image.jpg. No external fetch is available at tier 0.

expected: Social image should exist, be accessible, and meet minimum dimensions (1200x630 recommended).
recommendation: Verify the image exists and is at least 1200x630 px. Add og:image:width, og:image:height, and a fallback image hosted on the same origin.

expected_impact.rationale: Missing or incorrectly sized social images produce poor share previews, reducing engagement.

title: No photographic or illustrative images are present on the page

evidence.observed: No , , or

expected: Local-business landing pages typically include team, office, or client-work imagery with descriptive alt text.
recommendation: Consider adding relevant images with descriptive alt attributes. If intentionally omitted, this is a stylistic choice.

expected_impact.rationale: Lack of images may reduce visual engagement and E-E-A-T signals for a local service business.

title: Embedded Google Maps iframe uses lazy loading

evidence.observed: Footer Google Maps iframe includes loading="lazy" and a descriptive title.

expected: Third-party iframes should be lazy-loaded to avoid blocking initial page render.
recommendation: Maintain as-is. Consider adding a placeholder or facade for further performance improvement.

expected_impact.rationale: Lazy loading the map defers non-critical third-party resources, improving initial render performance.

title: Internal links reference non-existent paths

evidence.observed: Root-relative paths /portfolio, /agent-operativita, /automazione-ai, /privacy-policy, /cookie-policy, /termini-condizioni and others are linked but return 404.

expected: Every internal link should resolve to an existing page returning HTTP 200.
recommendation: Create missing pages or convert links to in-page anchors. Legal pages must exist and be reachable.

verification.reproduce: for path in /portfolio /agent-operativita /privacy-policy /cookie-policy /termini-condizioni; do curl -o /dev/null -s -w "${path}: %{http_code}\n" https://sarconx.com$path; done

expected_impact.rationale: Broken links waste crawl budget, leak PageRank to 404s, and harm user trust.

title: Social media footer links use empty href="#" anchors

evidence.observed: LinkedIn, Instagram, and Twitter/X icons in the footer link to href="#" despite JSON-LD sameAs referencing linkedin.com/company/sarconx and instagram.com/sarconx.

expected: Social profile links should point to the real brand profiles.
recommendation: Replace href="#" with actual profile URLs or remove the icons if profiles do not exist.

expected_impact.rationale: Dummy social links create broken UX and reduce trust signals; real profiles support E-E-A-T.

title: Primary page sections are linked via in-page anchors

evidence.observed: Navigation and CTA links use valid fragment identifiers (#servizi, #about, #dati, #agenti, #contatti).

expected: Single-page sites should use anchor links to navigate between sections.
recommendation: Maintain anchor links; ensure each target id exists.

expected_impact.rationale: Anchor links improve UX and allow search engines to identify distinct page sections.

title: Core Web Vitals metrics require PageSpeed Insights or CrUX API

evidence.observed: Tier 0 environment; no PSI, CrUX, or runtime performance data is available. Render-blocking external styles.css and Google Fonts CSS, plus an external Iconify script, are present in the head.

expected: LCP, INP, and CLS should meet the 'good' thresholds (LCP <2.5 s, INP <200 ms, CLS <0.1).
recommendation: Run PageSpeed Insights and CrUX to obtain field and lab metrics. Meanwhile, inline critical CSS, preload key fonts, defer non-critical scripts, and self-host fonts.

expected_impact.rationale: Core Web Vitals are a confirmed ranking factor for mobile search; poor performance can reduce rankings and increase bounce rates.

title: XML sitemap is missing

evidence.observed: sitemap.xml returns 404; no sitemap is linked from robots.txt or the page.

expected: A valid XML sitemap should exist at /sitemap.xml listing all indexable URLs and referenced from robots.txt.
recommendation: Create /sitemap.xml containing https://sarconx.com/ and any other live pages. Reference it in /robots.txt.

expected_impact.rationale: A sitemap accelerates discovery and indexing, especially for a new or low-authority domain.

title: WebSite SearchAction points to a non-existent site search

evidence.observed: WebSite entity declares SearchAction with target 'https://sarconx.it/?s={search_term_string}'. The site has no search form or search results page.

expected: SearchAction markup should only be present when a working search-results page exists at the declared target.
recommendation: Remove the SearchAction block, or implement a real search engine and verify it returns results before marking it up.

verification.assertion: WebSite potentialAction SearchAction is absent or points to a working search endpoint
verification.reproduce: node -e "const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const s=h.indexOf('<script type=\"application/ld+json\">'); const e=h.indexOf('</script>',s); const g=JSON.parse(h.slice(s+35,e))['@graph']; const ws=g.find(n=>n['@type']==='WebSite'); console.log(ws.potentialAction)"

expected_impact.rationale: Invalid SearchAction can mislead search engines and may be ignored or penalized in structured-data evaluation.

title: Customer testimonials are not marked up as Review entities

evidence.observed: Page contains three 5-star testimonials with quotes and author initials, but the @graph has no Review entities.

expected: If testimonials are genuine and consent is obtained, they should be expressed as Review markup.
recommendation: Add Review nodes for each testimonial with author Person, reviewRating, reviewBody and publisher SarconX. Obtain full reviewer names and consent.

verification.assertion: Review node count matches visible testimonials
verification.reproduce: node -e "const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const s=h.indexOf('<script type=\"application/ld+json\">'); const e=h.indexOf('</script>',s); const g=JSON.parse(h.slice(s+35,e))['@graph']; console.log('Review nodes:', g.filter(n=>n['@type']==='Review').length)"

expected_impact.rationale: Review markup provides social proof context to search and AI systems, supporting local business credibility.

title: Service entity lacks a required name property

evidence.observed: The Service node has @type, serviceType, provider, areaServed and hasOfferCatalog but no name property.

expected: Every Service entity should include a human-readable name.
recommendation: Add a name property such as 'Servizi SarconX' or 'AI Automation Services' to the Service node.

verification.reproduce: node -e "const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const s=h.indexOf('<script type=\"application/ld+json\">'); const e=h.indexOf('</script>',s); const g=JSON.parse(h.slice(s+35,e))['@graph']; const svc=g.find(n=>n['@type']==='Service'); console.log('Service name:', svc.name)"

expected_impact.rationale: A missing name weakens the Service entity's ability to match service-related queries.

title: LocalBusiness aggregateRating lacks verifiable on-page evidence

evidence.observed: LocalBusiness declares aggregateRating ratingValue 4.9, reviewCount 47. The @graph contains no Review entities, only three anonymous testimonials, and no link to a third-party review platform.

expected: AggregateRating should be supported by real, accessible reviews or a verified external profile to comply with structured-data guidelines.
recommendation: Either add Review markup for visible testimonials and source the 47 reviews from a real dataset, or remove aggregateRating until evidence is published.

verification.assertion: aggregateRating is either removed or backed by Review nodes or external review platform link
verification.reproduce: node -e "const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const s=h.indexOf('<script type=\"application/ld+json\">'); const e=h.indexOf('</script>',s); const g=JSON.parse(h.slice(s+35,e))['@graph']; const lb=g.find(n=>Array.isArray(n['@type'])?n['@type'].includes('LocalBusiness'):n['@type']==='LocalBusiness'); console.log('aggregateRating:', lb.aggregateRating); console.log('Review nodes:', g.filter(n=>n['@type']==='Review').length)"

expected_impact.rationale: Unsubstantiated ratings can be ignored by search quality systems and may risk rich-result penalties.

title: LocalBusiness missing hasMap, vatID and contactPoint

evidence.observed: Footer exposes P.IVA 02838240188, a Google Maps embed, and WhatsApp/phone/email. LocalBusiness has address/geo but no hasMap, vatID, or contactPoint.

expected: LocalBusiness should include hasMap, vatID/taxID, and a contactPoint to strengthen local-panel and entity signals.
recommendation: Add hasMap linking to the Google Maps URL, vatID 'IT02838240188', and a contactPoint for customer service.

verification.assertion: hasMap, vatID and contactPoint are present in LocalBusiness
verification.reproduce: node -e "const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const s=h.indexOf('<script type=\"application/ld+json\">'); const e=h.indexOf('</script>',s); const g=JSON.parse(h.slice(s+35,e))['@graph']; const lb=g.find(n=>Array.isArray(n['@type'])?n['@type'].includes('LocalBusiness'):n['@type']==='LocalBusiness'); console.log({hasMap: lb.hasMap, vatID: lb.vatID, contactPoint: lb.contactPoint})"

expected_impact.rationale: These fields strengthen local business verification and improve chances of appearing in local panels and AI entity summaries.

title: Homepage lacks visible and schema publication/update dates

evidence.observed: No datePublished or dateModified fields are present in the WebPage schema; no visible <time> element or 'Ultimo aggiornamento' text appears in the body.

expected: The WebPage JSON-LD should include datePublished and dateModified, and a visible last-updated date should be rendered on the page.
recommendation: Add datePublished and dateModified to the WebPage schema and display an 'Ultimo aggiornamento: [data]' line in the footer.

verification.assertion: datePublished or dateModified is present in WebPage schema and a visible update date is present
verification.reproduce: grep -P '(?i)date(Published|Modified)' index.html && grep -P '(?i)ultimo aggiornamento|pubblicato il' index.html

expected_impact.rationale: Freshness signals help search engines assess page currency and may improve crawl prioritization.

title: No named authors, founders, or verifiable team credentials

evidence.observed: About section states 'SarconX nasce dall'idea di due cofounder' but provides no names, photos, LinkedIn profiles, or bios. Claims such as '10+ Anni Industria' are generic and unattributed.

expected: Named founders/team members with job titles, relevant experience, certifications, and links to credible profiles.
recommendation: Add a Team or expanded 'Chi Siamo' section with real names, headshots, professional bios, and LinkedIn links; add founder/author schema entities.

verification.assertion: Founder/author names and credentials are present in body and schema

expected_impact.rationale: For a B2B service with ROI claims, named expertise and authority are core E-E-A-T signals.

title: Self-reported performance claims lack methodology or case-study evidence

evidence.observed: Trust badges claim '+300% Efficienza Operativa Media', '47 Giorni ROI Medio', '95% Riduzione Errori Umani'. FAQ repeats these figures. No methodology, sample-size disclosure, or case-study links support them.

expected: Quantified performance claims should cite methodology, sample size, time period, and link to anonymized case studies.
recommendation: Add a Case Study page, footnotes with sample size/period, or link each badge to a relevant project result.

verification.assertion: Performance badges and FAQ ROI claims have source or methodology link
verification.reproduce: grep -n -A1 -B1 'Efficienza Operativa Media\|Giorni ROI Medio\|Riduzione Errori Umani' index.html

expected_impact.rationale: Unsupported marketing claims weaken trust, increase scrutiny risk, and reduce conversion credibility.

title: Legal business name inconsistent between schema and privacy policy

evidence.observed: Schema LocalBusiness legalName is 'SarconX'. /privacy-policy page lists data controller as 'SarconX S.r.l.' with P.IVA 02838240188.

expected: Schema legalName should match the official registered name 'SarconX S.r.l.'.
recommendation: Update schema legalName to 'SarconX S.r.l.' and add it to the footer with registered office/REA if available.

verification.assertion: Schema legalName equals the official legal name

expected_impact.rationale: Consistent legal identity strengthens entity recognition in Google's Knowledge Graph and local business trust.

title: FAQ section provides self-contained, question-shaped answer blocks

evidence.observed: Page contains six <details>/<summary> FAQ items and a matching JSON-LD FAQPage with Question/Answer pairs.

expected: FAQ content should remain question-shaped, self-contained, and marked up with FAQPage schema.
recommendation: Add id attributes to each <details> element and expose anchor links so specific answers can be cited directly.

verification.assertion: FAQ details elements and FAQPage schema are present

expected_impact.rationale: Question-answer pairs in both HTML and schema are highly extractable and likely to be surfaced as direct answers by AI search engines.

title: Service cards are injected by JavaScript and absent from static HTML

evidence.observed: Services slider container is empty in raw HTML. The six service cards and their descriptions are only defined inside inline JavaScript and rendered at runtime.

expected: Core service content should be present in the initial HTML so static crawlers and AI retrieval snapshots can extract it.
recommendation: Server-side render the service cards, or add a static <noscript> list of services inside the slider container.

verification.assertion: raw_html contains service card content inside #serviceTrack
verification.reproduce: grep 'Automazione Flussi AI' index.html && grep 'serviceTrack'>' index.html

expected_impact.rationale: AI crawlers that do not execute JS may miss primary service descriptions, reducing retrieval coverage.

title: Industry statistics are numerous and include source attribution

evidence.observed: Data cards cite PwC, Osservatorio AI - Politecnico di Milano 2024, McKinsey, Accenture, and Gartner with labels.

expected: Continue providing authoritative, dated source labels for every third-party statistic and ideally link to the original report.
recommendation: Turn each source label into an outbound hyperlink to the original study or report.

expected_impact.rationale: Cited statistics provide factual anchors that make the page a strong candidate for AI citation.

title: First-party performance claims lack methodology or case-study support

evidence.observed: Trust badges and testimonials cite +300%, 47 giorni, 95%, 35%, 180%, €240.000 with no visible methodology, sample size, dates, or case-study links.

expected: First-party performance claims should be supported by methodology, sample size, time period, and/or links to detailed case studies.
recommendation: Create a 'Risultati Clienti' page with anonymized case studies and link the trust badges/testimonials to it.

verification.assertion: First-party claims have methodology or case-study links

expected_impact.rationale: AI engines are cautious about citing unsubstantiated marketing claims; without evidence these numbers may be ignored or reduce trust.

title: Robots meta tag is permissive for indexing and AI snippets

evidence.observed: <meta name="robots" content="index, follow, max-snippet:-1, max-image-preview:large, max-video-preview:-1">. No X-Robots-Tag blocking or noindex directive.

expected: The page should remain indexable and allow AI engines to extract snippets, images, and video previews.
recommendation: Maintain current robots meta. Consider adding a permissive robots.txt that explicitly allows major AI crawlers.

expected_impact.rationale: Permissive robots meta removes a basic barrier to crawling and snippet extraction by both search engines and AI bots.

title: CDN-level AI crawler access cannot be verified from snapshot

evidence.observed: Response headers show only Content-Type and Server: Netlify. robots.txt is 404. CDN or WAF rules filtering AI user-agents cannot be inspected from a standard fetch.

expected: All major AI crawlers should receive HTTP 200 for the HTML page and critical assets, with no challenge page or block.
recommendation: Test fetches using each AI crawler user-agent; publish a robots.txt that explicitly allows AI crawlers.

verification.assertion: AI crawler user-agents receive HTTP 200 with no challenge

expected_impact.rationale: If CDN or WAF blocks AI-specific user-agents, the page cannot be retrieved or cited regardless of content quality.

title: sameAs links are limited to LinkedIn and Instagram

evidence.observed: LocalBusiness sameAs lists linkedin.com/company/sarconx and instagram.com/sarconx. No Google Business Profile, Crunchbase, or other authoritative entity references.

expected: sameAs should reference verified, canonical profiles that help disambiguate the entity, especially a Google Business Profile tied to the Pavia address.
recommendation: Add a Google Business Profile URL and any verified industry-directory profiles. Ensure LinkedIn and Instagram URLs resolve to public, indexed pages.

verification.assertion: sameAs includes GBP and other verified profiles

expected_impact.rationale: Additional authoritative sameAs links strengthen entity disambiguation and can improve local and AI retrieval confidence.

title: No llms.txt file or link discovered

evidence.observed: llms.txt returns 404. The HTML <head> contains no <link rel="llms-txt"> tag.

expected: The site should provide an /llms.txt file with concise business context, services, contact information, and content guidelines.
recommendation: Create /llms.txt and add <link rel="llms-txt" href="/llms.txt"> in the <head>. Keep it factual and citation-friendly.

verification.assertion: GET /llms.txt returns 200 and head contains llms-txt link

expected_impact.rationale: llms.txt gives AI crawlers an explicit, low-noise signal about the business and services, increasing retrieval and citation accuracy.

title: No editorial, correction, or content-review policy disclosed

evidence.observed: No links to an editorial policy, correction policy, or content-review process appear in the footer or About section.

expected: A page or footer link describing how content is created, reviewed, sourced, and corrected.
recommendation: Add a brief 'Metodologia e Standard Editoriali' page linked in the footer.

verification.assertion: Editorial or correction policy link/text is found

expected_impact.rationale: Editorial transparency signals are increasingly used by AI overview systems to gauge source reliability.
